stimulus.content: In a free enterprise system, people can start their own businesses. They choose what to make and how much to charge. The government does not tell them what to produce. Buyers are free to choose which products they want to purchase.

stem: Which TWO features are part of a free enterprise system as described in this passage?

choices.text: People can start their own businesses
The government sets all prices for goods
Buyers are free to choose what they buy
Workers must make only what the state says

answer_explanation: In a free enterprise system, individuals have the freedom to start businesses and consumers have the freedom to choose what they buy. The government does not control prices or tell people what to produce. These features distinguish free enterprise from command economies.
